items.title: CVE-2026-35616: FortiClient EMS Pre-Auth API Bypass (CVSS 9.1) Actively Exploited
items.summary: Fortinet patched CVE-2026-35616, a pre-authentication API access bypass in FortiClient EMS that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to escalate privileges to SYSTEM level. The flaw carries a CVSS score of 9.1 and has been confirmed under active exploitation in the wild.
items.why: No credentials required to exploit — any internet-exposed FortiClient EMS instance is a direct path to full system compromise. FortiClient EMS is widely deployed for endpoint management in enterprise networks, making this a high-priority patch.

items.title: CVE-2026-2441: Chrome Zero-Day RCE Actively Exploited in the Wild
items.summary: Google patched CVE-2026-2441, a critical zero-day in Chrome that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code within the browser's sandbox. The vulnerability is being actively exploited in the wild with public reports of targeted attacks prior to the patch being available.
items.why: Chrome's multi-billion user base makes any actively exploited zero-day extremely high impact. Sandbox RCE as an initial foothold is a key stage in browser-based attack chains; unpatched endpoints are vulnerable to drive-by compromise via malicious sites.

items.title: APT28 (Fancy Bear) Weaponizes Microsoft Office CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours, Targets European Military
items.summary: Trellix researchers uncovered a stealthy multi-stage APT28 campaign that weaponized a newly disclosed Microsoft Office vulnerability (CVE-2026-21509) within 24 hours of public disclosure. The operation targets European military and government entities and uses cloud-based C2 infrastructure to blend into legitimate traffic.
items.why: A 24-hour weaponization window by a Tier-1 Russian nation-state actor against NATO-aligned targets illustrates that the effective patch window is now measured in hours, not days. Cloud C2 abuse significantly complicates network-level detection for defenders.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: PROMPTFLUX & PROMPTSTEAL Malware Query Live LLMs Mid-Execution to Evade Detection
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report documents two novel malware families — PROMPTFLUX and PROMPTSTEAL — that query large language models mid-execution to dynamically generate evasion code, bypassing static and behavioral detection. The report also tracked 714 new malware families in 2025, up from 632 in 2024, bringing the total tracked to over 6,000.
items.why: Malware that adapts its evasion logic in real time by calling out to LLMs represents a fundamentally new detection challenge. Signature-based and many behavior-based controls are rendered ineffective against payloads that rewrite themselves on each execution.

items.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: eCrime Breakout Time Drops to 29 Minutes, AI Adversaries Up 89%
items.summary: CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report reveals the average eCrime breakout time has fallen to 29 minutes — 65% faster than 2024 — with the fastest observed breakout at just 27 seconds. AI-enabled adversary activity increased 89% year-over-year. Russia-nexus FANCY BEAR deployed LAMEHUG, an LLM-enabled malware used to automate reconnaissance and document exfiltration.
items.why: Sub-30-minute lateral movement makes manual incident response functionally obsolete. The 89% rise in AI-enabled attacks combined with nation-state actors deploying LLM-powered malware signals that AI-assisted offense has crossed from theory into mainstream adversary tradecraft.
